title: Version still 1.0.4 (already on PyPI) with a large [Unreleased] changelog set

impact: Re-publishing without a bump would collide with the published 1.0.4 on PyPI
recommended_action: Bump version + finalize CHANGELOG at release (Section 6 plan, Section 9 execute)
validation: Section 6 decides target version; user bumps at release

title: ocman.py monolith continues to grow (~8.7k lines; +879 in delta)
evidence: single-module CLI by deliberate KISS design (ARCHITECTURE 'monolith is a deliberate trade-off'); delta added 4 feature areas + confirm seam
impact: Larger surface to navigate; but intentional per stated principle
recommended_action: No split now (would be a broad refactor); revisit only if a natural seam emerges
validation: Defer per Complexity axis; carry-in from prior S2-M1

title: No direct unit test for _per_project_disk_usage (per-project disk breakdown)
evidence: grep: no test references _per_project_disk_usage; dir_usage + info Backups section are tested
impact: The --by-project/disk output path is exercised only end-to-end, not unit-verified for correct per-project bytes/counts
recommended_action: Add a small test with a temp DB + fake session-diff files asserting rows/bytes

title: No direct unit test for confirm_destructive / _project_for_cwd
evidence: confirm_destructive behavior covered indirectly via cli_clean_backups cancel/dry-run tests; _project_for_cwd untested
impact: Seam and cwd->project mapping lack focused tests; low risk (simple, fail-soft)
recommended_action: Optionally add focused unit tests for confirm_destructive (yes/dry_run/assume_yes/EOF) and _project_for_cwd

title: Bare pytest can resolve an installed ocman instead of local source
evidence: README documents PYTHONPATH=. ; CI sets PYTHONPATH=.; local env has editable install (mitigated)
impact: Fresh clone with non-editable install could test wrong package
recommended_action: Already documented; optional conftest/pytest.ini to pin rootdir
validation: Carry-in; keep documented, optional pytest.ini in S7
